runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Ruby", "build_system": "bundler", "package_manager": "bundler", "build_commands": ["bundle install"], "install_commands": ["apk add --no-cache build-base ruby-dev libffi-dev"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "3.4.9", "system_packages": ["build-base", "ruby-dev", "libffi-dev"]}, "repo_type": "web_service", "base_image": "ruby:3.4.9-alpine3.23", "base_image_rationale": "Official Ruby image on Alpine for a minimal footprint suitable for a Rack-based web app.", "pitfalls": ["Alpine-based Ruby images may require build tools for native gems; ensure a Gemfile exists or vendor dependencies with bundle install --path vendor/bundle.", "Gem native extensions may fail to build if required libraries are missing (e.g., zlib, openssl, libxml2); additional libs may be needed depending on gems used."], "notes": "This app appears to be a Rack-based Ruby web app serving static HTML and dynamic resume data. The Dockerfile should install dependencies via Bundler and run rackup to serve the app, exposing port 9292. If Gemfile is absent, consider adding a minimal Gemfile with rack and bundler to ensure predictable builds."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
FROM ruby:3.4.9-alpine3.23

# Install build dependencies for native gems
RUN apk add --no-cache build-base ruby-dev libffi-dev

# Set work directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy project files
COPY . .

# If Gemfile is not present in the repo, create a minimal Gemfile for predictable Bundler behavior
RUN set -e; if [ ! -f Gemfile ]; then printf "source 'https://rubygems.org'\n\ngem 'rack'\n" > Gemfile; fi

# Ensure Bundler is available and install dependencies
RUN gem install bundler
RUN bundle config set path 'vendor/bundle'
RUN bundle install

# Expose Rack port
EXPOSE 9292

# Run the Rack app
CMD ["bundle", "exec", "rackup", "-o", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "9292"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM ruby:3.4.9-alpine3.23

# Install build dependencies for native gems
RUN apk add --no-cache build-base ruby-dev libffi-dev

# Set work directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy project files
COPY . .

# If Gemfile is not present in the repo, create a minimal Gemfile for predictable Bundler behavior
RUN set -e; if [ ! -f Gemfile ]; then printf "source 'https://rubygems.org'\n\ngem 'rack'\n" > Gemfile; fi

# Ensure Bundler is available and install dependencies
RUN gem install bundler
RUN bundle install --path vendor/bundle

# Expose Rack port
EXPOSE 9292

# Run the Rack app
CMD ["bundle", "exec", "rackup", "-o", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "9292"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "# Ignore things that aren't needed in the build context
.git
node_modules
vendor/bundle
.DS_Store
*.log

"}

artifacts.content:
FROM ruby:3.4.9-alpine3.23

# Install build dependencies for native gems
RUN apk add --no-cache build-base ruby-dev libffi-dev

# Set work directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy project files
COPY . .

# If Gemfile is not present in the repo, create a minimal Gemfile for predictable Bundler behavior
RUN set -e; if [ ! -f Gemfile ]; then printf "source 'https://rubygems.org'\n\ngem 'rack'\n" > Gemfile; fi

# Ensure Bundler is available and install dependencies
RUN gem install bundler
RUN bundle config set path 'vendor/bundle'
RUN bundle install

# Expose Rack port
EXPOSE 9292

# Run the Rack app
CMD ["bundle", "exec", "rackup", "-o", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "9292"]
